# Tokcat

> Tokcat is a native macOS menu-bar app that visualizes your AI coding token usage — Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Gemini, Copilot, Amp, Droid, Hermes — as a 2D or 3D GitHub-style contribution graph. Built with Tauri 2 (Rust + React/Vite), distributed via Homebrew and GitHub Releases. Apple Silicon, macOS 11+. MIT licensed.

## Install

- [Homebrew](https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat#quick-start): `brew tap handlecusion/tokcat && brew install --cask tokcat`
- [DMG download](https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/releases/latest)
- [Homebrew tap repository](https://github.com/handlecusion/homebrew-tokcat)

## Documentation

- [README (English)](https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/blob/main/README.md)
- [README (Korean)](https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/blob/main/README.ko-KR.md)
- [Landing page](https://handlecusion.github.io/tokcat/)
- [Releases](https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/releases)

## Architecture

- [Tauri shell source](https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/tree/main/src-tauri): Rust backend, native tray, animation frames
- [Frontend source](https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/tree/main/src): React + Vite, react-three-fiber 3D graph
- [tokscale upstream](https://github.com/junhoyeo/tokscale): reference project for supported local log formats

## Supported AI clients

Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Google Gemini, GitHub Copilot, Amp, Droid, Hermes, and compatible local logs.

## Privacy

No telemetry. No cloud sync. No account. The only network request is the GitHub Releases updater manifest checked every 30 minutes. Token data is read locally from session logs.
